entities.name: diamond system
entities.description: A mathematical aiming system used in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: 3-cushion billiards
entities.description: A variant of billiards where the cue ball must contact three cushions before pocketing an object ball.

entities.name: diamond markers
entities.description: Markers on the rails used in the diamond system for aiming calculations.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The ball struck by the player to make shots in billiards.

entities.name: object ball
entities.description: A ball that must be pocketed during a shot in billiards.

entities.name: rebound angle
entities.description: The angle at which a ball bounces off a cushion.

entities.name: spin
entities.description: A rotational motion imparted to a billiard ball.

entities.name: cloth conditions
entities.description: The state of the table's cloth, which can affect ball behavior.

entities.name: diamond system
entities.description: A method for aiming in billiards.

entities.name: billiard table
entities.description: The playing surface of a billiard game.

entities.name: diamonds
entities.description: Reference points on the rail for aiming in billiards.

entities.name: plus 2 system
entities.description: A numbering system used in diamond system aiming.

entities.name: plus 3 system
entities.description: Another numbering system used in diamond system aiming.

entities.name: long rails
entities.description: Rails on the billiard table where diamonds are numbered 1-7.

entities.name: short rails
entities.description: Rails on the billiard table where diamonds are numbered 1-3.

entities.name: plus 2 formula
entities.description: A diamond system pattern in 3-cushion billiards where the starting and ending diamond numbers add up to 2.

entities.name: diamond system
entities.description: A method used in 3-cushion billiards to predict the path of the cue ball based on the positions of the diamonds and object balls.

entities.name: long patterns
entities.description: Shots where the cue ball travels a significant distance across the table, typically involving contact with both long rails.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The small white ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: short rail
entities.description: One of the rails on a billiard table, typically shorter than the long rails.

entities.name: long rail
entities.description: One of the longer rails on a billiard table, used in various shots.

entities.name: object balls
entities.description: The colored balls used in billiards besides the cue ball.

entities.name: plus 3
entities.description: A formula used in billiards where the starting diamond number plus the ending diamond number equals 3.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: object balls
entities.description: Balls that are struck by the cue ball during a shot in billiards.

entities.name: diamonds
entities.description: Markers on the table used to indicate positions for balls in billiards.

entities.name: long rail
entities.description: One of the long sides of the billiard table.

entities.name: short rail
entities.description: The other long side of the billiard table.

entities.name: corner
entities.description: One of the four corners of the billiard table.

entities.name: natural pattern
entities.description: A standard shot in billiards where the cue ball travels from one rail to another, hitting an object ball and then a rail.

entities.name: Cross-table shots
entities.description: A type of shot in 3-cushion billiards where the cue ball travels from one short rail to another, contacting one or more long rails.

entities.name: Diamond system
entities.description: A numbering system used for cross-table shots in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: Short rail
entities.description: One of the rails on a billiard table, typically shorter than the long rails.

entities.name: Long rail
entities.description: One of the rails on a billiard table, typically longer than the short rails.

entities.name: Cue ball
entities.description: The white ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: Object ball
entities.description: Any of the colored balls used in billiards, except the cue ball.

entities.name: Drill
entities.description: A practice exercise for billiards.

entities.name: Straight Plus-2 Patterns
entities.description: A drill focusing on shooting plus-2 patterns in a straight line.

entities.name: Angled Plus-2 Patterns
entities.description: A drill focusing on shooting plus-2 patterns at an angle.

entities.name: Plus-3 Patterns
entities.description: A drill focusing on shooting plus-3 patterns.

entities.name: Mixed Patterns
entities.description: A drill involving random positions and mixed systems.

entities.name: Diamond System
entities.description: A system for calculating shot angles in billiards.

entities.name: Cue Ball
entities.description: The ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: Object Balls
entities.description: Balls that are struck by the cue ball in billiards.

entities.name: Diamonds
entities.description: Markers on the table used for aiming shots in billiards.

entities.name: diamond system
entities.description: A powerful tool for billiards that helps players make accurate shots.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The white ball used to strike other balls in billiards.

entities.name: object ball
entities.description: Any of the colored balls used in billiards as targets for the cue ball.

entities.name: cushion
entities.description: The rail around the table that the cue ball and object balls hit during play.

entities.name: spin
entities.description: A method of applying rotation to the cue ball to change its trajectory upon impact with another ball or cushion.

entities.name: extreme angles
entities.description: Angles greater than 60 degrees when striking the cue ball on a cushion, making it less predictable.

entities.name: short shots
entities.description: Shots where the cue ball is very close to the first object ball, making deflection angles difficult to calculate.

entities.name: long shots
entities.description: Shots where the cue ball needs to travel a long distance across the table, amplifying small errors in aiming.

entities.name: experience and 'feel'
entities.description: A player's ability to intuitively adjust shots based on specific conditions and ball positions.

entities.name: table conditions
entities.description: Factors such as cloth speed, cushion hardness, and humidity that affect shot accuracy.

entities.name: intuition
entities.description: A player's ability to make subtle adjustments based on their understanding of the game.
